_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: A triangle has a base of 12 cm and a corresponding height of 5 cm. What is the area of the triangle, in square centimeters?
author_rationale: Triangle area = 1/2 × base × height = 1/2 × 12 × 5 = 30 cm². The stem states the base and its corresponding perpendicular height explicitly, so there is exactly one correct value. Distractor 60 forgets the 1/2 factor; 17 = 12 + 5 adds base and height; 34 = 2 × (12 + 5) treats them as rectangle sides.
